steps.title: Complete the square
steps.content:
Rewrite the circle equation by grouping the $x$-terms and $y$-terms.
$$
x^2-6x+y^2-4y-51=0
$$
$$
(x-3)^2+(y-2)^2=64
$$
So the radius is $8$.

steps.title: Use the square side
steps.content: For a circle inscribed in a square, the square's side length equals the circle's diameter. $$ \text{side}=2(8)=16 $$ $$ \text{perimeter}=4(16)=64 $$ The answer is C.
